rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 13, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF FIRESIDE THRIFT CO.'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption The motion is transferred to be heard in Department 302 at 9:30 a.m. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: FIRESIDE THRIFT CO. vs. MCDOWELL, CHRISTY A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Fireside Thrift Co. Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, Line 19, Plaintiff Fireside Thrift Co.'s Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Denied. Judgment debtor has not presented evidence sufficient to show that all of her income is necessary for her support. Judgment debtor shall continue to pay $100 per month towards the judgment. $100 of the funds being held shall be released to the judgment creditor. The remaining amount shall be released to the judgment debtor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK MALKASSIAN et al VS. HOWARD MULLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT GAVIN PAINTING CONSTRUCTION, INC., DBA GAVIN CONSTRUCTION'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination. The motion is granted. The record contains substantial evidence demonstrating that the settlement is in good faith within the meaning of Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Assoc., (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Defendant fails to present any proof showing that the settlement lacks good faith and does not meet its burden under CCP 877.6(d). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the moving party without need for any appearances. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GCFS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOEL S RUTLEDGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 13, 2012, Line 18, PLAINTIFF GCFS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Plaintiff has failed to produce admissible evidence to support its first cause of action for breach of contract. Defendant's evidentiary objections on grounds of authentication and hearsay to Exhibit 1 are sustained. Objection to Exhibit 2 is sustained; no foundation for the credit card statements. Plaintiff thus did not meet its burden of production to make a prima facie showing that there are no triable issues of material fact. Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield, 25 Cal. 4th 826, 850 (2001). Further, because Plaintiff moved for summary judgment only, the court need not reach the second cause of action. There can be no summary adjudication where the notice of motion did not request it. The Court did not receive a copy of Defendant's separate written objections to evidence, which are referred to in its opposition papers and in reply. If there is argument on this motion, Defendant's counsel is requested to bring a copy of the separate written objections to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROTH RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, INC. VS. IVONNE CORTEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Response To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 13, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 7, PLAINTIFF ROTH RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Response To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff ROTH RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion for Order Compelling Response to Discovery is GRANTED. Plaintiff ROTH RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Request for Sanctions in the amount of $775.00 is GRANTED. Order signed by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
